question.text: A call option is said to be 'In the Money' (ITM) when:

answer.text: Spot price of the underlying > Strike price
Spot price of the underlying < Strike price
Spot price of the underlying = Strike price

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Call ITM: Spot > Strike (exercising generates positive cash flow). Put ITM: Spot < Strike. ATM: Spot = Strike. OTM: opposite of ITM. **The Trap:** For PUT options, ITM is when Spot < Strike — the opposite of calls. **Cross-Exam Context:** Moneyness classification is tested in all options-related exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Only ITM options have intrinsic value. ATM and OTM options have zero intrinsic value (their premium is entirely time value).
